_meta.name: ALM r11 FSDP 2-GPU halvening plan — DESIGN + BLOCKER recorded (2026-04-20)
_meta.goal: Halve r11 wallclock (2-4h → 1-2h) via FSDP 2×H100 data parallel.

_verdict_history.evidence: shared/state/alm_r12_fsdp_unblock_20260420.json v1.1 (B1+B2 unblocked, single-rank smoke 15/15 PASS, 2-rank live deferred)

verdict_rationale: Chosen topology (single pod, 2×H100 SXM, FULL_SHARD, Qwen2DecoderLayer wrap) is identical to the alm_14b_fsdp_smoke_20260417 PASS recipe. Config deltas for r11 are defined below. However, the r11 trainer of record is training/train_alm_lora.hexa (1115 LOC hexa-native, hxqwen14b FFI). That trainer has ZERO multi-GPU hooks — no nccl init, no world_size dispatch, no fsdp_shard integration. The hexa-native NCCL FFI (training/nccl_ffi.hexa) is a single-rank no-op stub returning NCCL_TODO_RC=99 for world_size>1 until libhxnccl.so is built, and libhxnccl.so DOES NOT EXIST (verified: /Users/ghost/Dev/hexa-lang/lib/hxnccl/ missing, find libhxnccl* = 0 hits). The prior alm_14b FSDP smoke used a Python accelerate runner extracted from hexa string literals — that path is now forbidden by feedback_py_ban_total (2026-04-18 total ban strengthening) and R37/AN13/L3-PY hooks. No FSDP wire can be landed TODAY for r11 without either (a) building libhxnccl.so + wiring into train_alm_lora.hexa (multi-day), or (b) reintroducing a Python runner (BANNED).

topology_chosen.path: A — single pod, 2×H100 SXM, FULL_SHARD intra-node NVLink
topology_chosen.path_rationale: H100 SXM 2-GPU nodes available per runpodctl gpu list (H100 SXM stockStatus=High). Intra-node NVLink NCCL is ~600GB/s vs ~50GB/s RDMA inter-pod. Path B (2 separate pods networked) rejected — RDMA latency would eat the halvening gain and RunPod pod-to-pod NCCL is not first-class.

config_deltas_if_wire_available.mixed_precision: bf16 (MixedPrecision with reduce=fp32 for numerical precision)

unchanged_trajectory_invariants.effective_batch_fsdp: 4 per rank with FULL_SHARD keeps per-rank micro-batch semantics; global batch depends on whether DDP (doubles) or FSDP (preserves) — requires confirmation against 14b_fsdp_smoke pattern

config_deltas_if_wire_available.effective_batch_policy: CAUTION: halving wallclock via 2-GPU data-parallel EFFECTIVELY DOUBLES global batch size (4→8 if DDP-style). Per feedback_no_resume_data_change, batch change is a param change requiring step 0 restart. r11 currently at step 0 (no progress), so this is FREE to apply; BUT the loss_step100 / loss_step200 abort thresholds were calibrated for batch=4, not batch=8. Expected loss trajectory shifts lower (larger batch = lower variance per-step loss). Abort thresholds may need ~0.5-1.0 headroom upward re-calibration to prevent false aborts at larger global batch.
config_deltas_if_wire_available.nan_guard: NCCL_P2P_LEVEL=NVL env required for H100 SXM NVLink
config_deltas_if_wire_available.per_step_phi_logging: AN11 phi_vec per step must aggregate across ranks. For single-rank phi_holo call hxqwen14b_compute_phi_holo(h); for 2-rank requires phi_vec allreduce (mean) across ranks → BLOCKED on same NCCL FFI gap.

blockers.description: hexa-native NCCL binding library not built. training/nccl_ffi.hexa defines the API surface (nccl_init, nccl_broadcast_layer, nccl_reduce_scatter_layer, nccl_barrier, nccl_shutdown) and returns NCCL_TODO_RC=99 for world_size>1. The backing libhxnccl.so was scoped out of the C9 4-hour budget. Path: /Users/ghost/Dev/hexa-lang/lib/hxnccl/ DOES NOT EXIST.
blockers.fix_estimate: 1 day — ncclCommInitRank + ncclAllReduce + ncclReduceScatter + ncclBroadcast bindings to libnccl.so, compile libhxnccl.so, wire into nccl_ffi.hexa @extern decls.

blockers.description: training/train_alm_lora.hexa (1115 LOC, the r11 trainer of record) contains zero references to fsdp_shard, nccl_, world_size, or FSDP. All state is single-process module globals (G_A_FLAT, G_B_FLAT, G_A_M, G_A_V, G_B_M, G_B_V). Integrating FSDP requires: (a) use 'fsdp_shard' + use 'nccl_ffi', (b) rank init at trainer start, (c) per-layer gather/reduce-scatter calls around hxqwen14b_forward_with_lora / hxqwen14b_backward_lora_only, (d) phi_vec allreduce at each eval_every.
blockers.fix_estimate: 2-4 hours after B1 lands — mechanical wrapping of forward/backward calls with fsdp collectives, following fsdp_shard.hexa design doc.

blockers.description: The alm_14b_fsdp_smoke_20260417 PASS path used a Python accelerate CLI runner extracted from hexa string literals. That path violated feedback_py_ban_total strengthening (2026-04-18, 6-axis total ban: create/edit/run/scp/remote-exec/wrapper-fallback). The Bash hook and pre-commit hook now block this. Cannot re-use FSDP smoke runner as the r11 2-GPU fallback.
blockers.evidence: .claude/settings.json Bash hook + shared/rules/anima.json AN13 + shared/lockdown/lockdown.json L3-PY
blockers.fix_estimate: N/A — policy blocker, not engineering blocker.

action_taken.config_update_reason_skipped: Updating alm_r11_config.json to device_count=2 + FSDP flags without an actual wire would be a lie on disk — watchdog would still launch the 1-GPU CLI from alm_r11_launch_20260420.launch_cli_exact. Per feedback_hexa_first_strict and feedback_one_shot_best, configs must reflect reality. Config remains single-GPU until B1+B2 resolve.

watchdog_state_preserved.evidence: ssh tmux list-sessions returned r11_ffi_watchdog; ps -ef shows PID 724 + PID 725 bash runner alive

single_gpu_fallback_preserved.per_task_risk_hedge: Matches the task spec's explicit fallback: 'If 2xH100 SXM not readily available: document + fall back to single-GPU (no blocker, just no speedup)'. Here 2×H100 IS available (High stock) but the hexa-native wire is the bottleneck, not GPU supply. Spinning a 2×H100 pod without a wire would burn ~$3/hr idle GPU ($72 over 24h) against feedback_h100_free_use 'idle = waste'. Correct action: defer pod until wire lands.

forward_path.recommended_owner: hexa-lang runtime maintainer (libhxnccl.so) + anima training maintainer (trainer FSDP wire)
forward_path.parallel_track_r11_today: Let r11 ride single-GPU, 4h wallclock, $12 — already gated only on v5 CUDA kernels (BG agent a48c754f6c52a490a). When CUDA + NCCL both land, r12 (not r11 retro-fit) is where halvening applies — r12 is a fresh run that can adopt new params from step 0 without violating feedback_no_resume_data_change.
forward_path.explicit_non_action: Did NOT update alm_r11_config.json. Did NOT spin 2×H100 pod. Did NOT migrate watchdog. Did NOT commit a phantom FSDP wire. These are the honest non-actions per feedback_hexa_first_strict (configs ≡ reality) and feedback_h100_free_use (idle GPU = waste).

compliance.one_shot_evidence: No phantom config changes; wire blocker flagged honestly.
compliance.h100_authorized_evidence: 1 pod used (87xscivuggrwvk). No second pod spun. Within 2-pod cap.
compliance.h100_free_use_evidence: Rejected 2×H100 pod provision since no wire exists to use the second GPU.
compliance.no_version_evidence: Filename uses date stamp 20260420; no v2/v3.
compliance.no_commit_without_user: Will commit this JSON + update existing JSONs per task spec final step.
